g.title: Disassembled soil sensor and alligator clips
g.desc: This diagram shows the wooden soil sensor and two alligator clip wires as separate components. The soil sensor is a flat piece of 5mm plywood shaped like a garden stake with a pointed bottom and a flat top. The two wires, one red and one black, end in spring-loaded alligator clips with smooth plastic covers over their metal jaws. The instructions illustrate that the clips should be unfastened from the top edge of the wooden sensor, leaving the parts completely disconnected.

g.title: Kit Inventory: Check Your Parts
g.desc: This section of the blueprint is the 'INVENTORY' list, which helps you identify and confirm all the parts included in your Piper Make Soil Sensor kit before you begin assembly. This particular step does not involve disassembly, but rather helps you verify that you have all the necessary components. Here are the items you should check for: * **1 Plastic Stake:** Feel for one smooth, rigid plastic piece, likely long and thin, possibly with a slightly pointed end at one side. * **1 Copper Tape:** Locate one roll or strip of thin, flexible, metallic tape that feels smooth and cool to the touch. * **1 Sticker:** Find one thin, flat piece with an adhesive backing, possibly with a slight raised edge where it peels off a larger sheet. * **2 Alligator Clips:** You should have two metal clips. Each will have a spring mechanism to open and close its 'jaws,' which have small teeth. The handles are often covered in a softer plastic or rubber material. * **1 Bar Graph LED:** This is a small electronic component, typically a rectangular plastic piece. You might feel several small, uniform bumps or flat surfaces arranged in a line on one face, which are the individual LEDs. Multiple thin metal 'legs' (pins) will extend from one side of this component. * **2 1M ohm Resistors:** Feel for two very small, cylindrical electronic components, each with two thin metal 'legs' extending from its ends. * **10 333 ohm Resistors:** You will find ten more of these small cylindrical electronic components, similar in size and feel to the 1M ohm resistors, each with two thin metal 'legs' extending from its ends. * **2 Aluminum Square Tape:** These are two individual pieces of thin, metallic tape, similar in feel to copper tape, but cut into distinct square shapes rather than being a continuous roll.

g.title: Disconnect Alligator Clips, Store Parts
g.desc: This is the first step for disassembling your kit. You will be removing parts and preparing them for storage. First, locate the long, flat soil sensor piece. It feels like 5mm thick plywood and has a pointed end at the bottom. It is currently standing upright in a smooth, clear glass of water. At the top edge of the soil sensor, you will find two alligator clips attached: one with a red plastic handle and one with a black plastic handle. These are metal clips with a spring action and have wires coming from them. To remove each clip, grasp its plastic handle, gently squeeze the handle to open the clip's jaws, and then pull it straight up and off the soil sensor. Do this for both the red and black clips. After removing both alligator clips, carefully lift the soil sensor out of the glass. Finally, place the soil sensor, the two alligator clips, and the empty glass into their specific labeled spots within your 'clean up box' for organized storage.

text.tspan: spot in the clean up box

g.title: Dispose of the Plastic Stake
g.desc: This step shows a long, flat plastic stake. It is shaped like a large craft stick but comes to a sharp point at one end. You can identify it by its flat, smooth plastic texture and its pointed tip. The instruction is to dispose of this piece, as it is no longer needed for the assembly.

g.title: Completion of the Disassembly Process
g.desc: This instruction step consists of text on a blue background indicating that disassembly is complete. It features the bold headline 'DISASSEMBLY COMPLETE!' and a secondary instruction box that directs you to place all separated plywood pieces and brass hardware into their designated spots in the clean-up area for organized storage.

text.tspan: each piece on its spot in

g.title: Organize Clips, LED, and Resistors
g.desc: This section shows four groups of electronic parts for sorting. First, find two flexible alligator clip wires, recognizable by the spring-loaded metal clips at each end. Second, locate a single Bar Graph LED, which is a small rectangular block with ten tiny rectangular segments on its top face and two rows of metal pins underneath. Finally, identify two types of resistors: small cylindrical beads with a thin wire poking out from each end. Group the two 1M Ohm resistors together and the ten 333 Ohm resistors separately; they are tactilely identical but have different colored stripes.

g.title: Set of Two Alligator Clip Wires
g.desc: Locate two flexible, insulated wires, one red and one black. Each wire has a spring-loaded metal clip at both ends. You can identify these by feeling for the soft plastic sleeve covering the clip; squeezing the back of this sleeve will open the metal 'jaws' at the tip, which have a serrated, tooth-like texture.

g.title: Bar Graph LED Component
g.desc: A small rectangular electronic component. Its top surface features a row of ten distinct, small rectangular LED segments. On the underside, you will feel two parallel rows of ten thin metal pins each. This part is approximately one inch long and half an inch wide.

g.title: Two 1M Ohm Resistors
g.desc: Find two 1M Ohm resistors. These are tiny electronic components, each featuring a hard, smooth, cylindrical center body about the size of a grain of rice. A very thin, flexible wire lead extends from each end of this central body. You can identify them tactually by their small size and the feel of the tiny bead-like center on a thin wire.

g.title: Ten 333 Ohm Resistors
g.desc: Locate ten resistors. Each resistor is a very small electronic component featuring a tiny, hard, cylindrical body about the size of a grain of rice. From each end of this central body, a long, thin, and flexible metal wire lead extends. Tactilely, you can identify them by the smooth, slightly rounded central 'bead' and the two thin, needle-like wires on either side. These components are non-polarized, so they don't have a specific top or bottom.

g.title: Disassembled soil sensor and alligator clips
g.desc: This diagram shows the wooden soil sensor and two alligator clip wires as separate components. The soil sensor is a flat piece of 5mm plywood shaped like a garden stake with a pointed bottom and a flat top. The two wires, one red and one black, end in spring-loaded alligator clips with smooth plastic covers over their metal jaws. The instructions illustrate that the clips should be unfastened from the top edge of the wooden sensor, leaving the parts completely disconnected.
